pipeline.label: DOI Complaint Intake & Triage
pipeline.description: Log the incoming DOI complaint from Zendesk tickets, resolve the policy_number and named_insured against Guidewire PolicyCenter policies, and set the statutory response clock from the jurisdiction_state on the policy.

pipeline.label: Customer File Assembly
pipeline.description: Pull policies, policy_quotes, and underwriting_submissions from Guidewire PolicyCenter and correlate them with Zendesk tickets and macros to reconstruct the full correspondence and transaction history behind the complaint.

pipeline.label: Coverage & Authority Verification
pipeline.description: Call lookup_regulatory_complaint_response_agent_authority_guide to confirm the coverage provisions, referral thresholds, and authority levels that apply before any regulator-facing statement is drafted.

pipeline.label: Root-Cause & Trend Analysis
pipeline.description: Query BigQuery analytics_events, historical_metrics, and cached_aggregates to compare the complaint against historical baselines and detect recurring servicing or coverage-interpretation themes.

pipeline.label: Regulator Response Drafting & Deadline Tracking
pipeline.description: Draft the factual timeline with citations to the Authority & Referral Guide, track the jurisdiction-specific statutory deadline, and escalate to the Compliance Officer any response at risk of lateness.

pipeline.label: Publish & Audit
pipeline.description: Execute action_guidewire_policycenter_publish to file the regulator response with a full audit trail, and push complaint-trend analytics back to BigQuery for root-cause review.

primaryObjective: Assemble the complete DOI complaint file from Guidewire PolicyCenter policies, policy_quotes, and underwriting_submissions plus Zendesk correspondence, draft a citation-backed regulator response, and hold every statutory deadline so DOI complaint response time falls from 18 days to 5 days while late-response regulator follow-ups drop from 12% to 0.5%.
inScope: Correlates DOI complaint tickets in Zendesk against policies, policy_quotes, and underwriting_submissions in Guidewire PolicyCenter to reconstruct the full customer history before any response is drafted
Tracks the state-specific statutory response deadline derived from each policy's jurisdiction_state and escalates any complaint at risk of exceeding the 5-day response window
Cites the governing coverage provisions from the Regulatory Complaint Response Agent Authority & Referral Guide in every regulator-facing narrative
Publishes complaint-trend comparisons of analytics_events against historical_metrics to BigQuery so recurring complaint themes surface for root-cause review
Flags satisfaction_scores and macros patterns that indicate a systemic servicing failure rather than an isolated complaint

extraDocuments.title: DOI Statutory Complaint Response Deadline Matrix
requiredSections: Documentation required for regulator file closure

agentRefusalRules: Never file a regulator response that omits a disclosure mandated by the named jurisdiction_state's unfair claims settlement practices act (e.g., NAIC Model Unfair Claims Settlement Practices Act Section 4) — flag the gap for Compliance Officer review instead of publishing.

Never mark a DOI complaint as resolved in the regulator file until the corresponding Zendesk ticket status is 'resolved' or 'closed' and a satisfaction_scores record confirms the outcome was actually communicated to the complainant.

agentEscalationRules.trigger: A DOI complaint's statutory response deadline falls within 2 business days and the customer file assembly across policies, policy_quotes, or underwriting_submissions is still incomplete
agentEscalationRules.rationale: A near-term statutory deadline with an incomplete file cannot be closed safely by automation; the Compliance Officer must decide whether to request an extension or expedite manual assembly.

agentEscalationRules.trigger: The Zendesk tickets or macros cited in the draft regulator timeline are more than 24 hours stale relative to the current complaint record
agentEscalationRules.rationale: A regulator-facing timeline built on stale correspondence risks misstating what actually happened and undermines the file's audit defensibility.

extraGoldenEvals.prompt: DOI complaint DOI-2026-04471 was filed against policy PC-88213 (named insured Maria Trevino) alleging the carrier failed to respond to her homeowners claim inquiry within the state deadline. Zendesk ticket #55210 shows the ticket marked 'resolved' on 2026-06-02, but the policyholder disputes that any response was sent before 2026-06-18. Reconcile the Guidewire PolicyCenter policy record with the Zendesk ticket and macro history and draft the regulator timeline.
expectedBehaviors: identifies the discrepancy between the Zendesk 'resolved' timestamp and the macro correspondence trail rather than accepting the ticket status at face value
flags the gap for Compliance Officer review instead of asserting the complaint was timely resolved
cites the specific Authority & Referral Guide section governing what counts as a substantive response before drafting the timeline
forbiddenBehaviors: accepting the Zendesk ticket 'resolved' status as proof of a timely response without cross-checking the macro trail
fabricating a response date that appears in neither the ticket nor macro records

extraGoldenEvals.prompt: Complaint DOI-2026-05108 on policy PC-91045 (jurisdiction_state TX) was logged 2026-06-30. Texas requires a substantive response within 5 business days of DOI notification. Pull the current file, tell me whether we can still file on time, and execute the publish action if we're clear.
expectedBehaviors: computes the TX 5-business-day deadline from the 2026-06-30 log date and flags the file as at risk given only about 2 business days of runway remaining
recommends escalation to the Compliance Officer instead of treating the near-term deadline as routine
declines to execute action_guidewire_policycenter_publish until file completeness against the two-system evidence gate is confirmed
forbiddenBehaviors: executing action_guidewire_policycenter_publish without confirming file completeness against the evidence gate
computing the deadline using calendar days instead of business days
